meta.description: Bug backlog from deep QA analysis. 6 confirmed bugs + 8 potential issues organized into 5 dependency-ordered phases.

bugs.title: Double ref.listen fires two state cycles per word advance
bugs.description: parallax_reading_screen.dart had two separate ref.listen calls on wordTimerProvider — one selecting currentIndex, one watching full state. Both fired on every word advance, causing double bookmark updates and doubled state propagation.

tasks.description: Consolidated two ref.listen calls into a single ref.listen<WordTimerState> that handles: _wordNotifier update, _sessionWordCount increment, bookmark persist, and range completion check.
Verified existing single ref.listen is already correct — no change needed.

bugs.title: wordTimerProvider is global singleton — shared across reading sessions
bugs.description: The global StateNotifierProvider was never disposed between reading sessions. If a user navigated from PDF A to PDF B, the previous timer state persisted until loadDocument() was called in the new screen, briefly showing stale words.

tasks.description: Changed wordTimerProvider from StateNotifierProvider to StateNotifierProvider.autoDispose so it cleans up when no reading screen is listening.
No changes needed — ref.watch in build() automatically keeps autoDispose provider alive while screen is mounted.
No changes needed — same as above.

bugs.title: _PremiumReadGuard rebuilds reading screen on every config change
bugs.description: The guard used ref.watch(configProvider) which triggered rebuilds on every bookmark update (every word advance). This propagated unnecessary rebuilds at reading-speed frequency through the widget tree.

tasks.description: Replaced ref.watch(configProvider) with ref.watch(configProvider.select((c) => c.valueOrNull?.hasPremium ?? false)) to rebuild only when premium status changes.

bugs.title: BookmarkNotifier._persist() in dispose() is fire-and-forget
bugs.description: _persist() is async but called from synchronous dispose(). The Future fires but the notifier tears down immediately, potentially losing the user's last reading position on fast exit or force-kill.

tasks.description: Added _lastPersistedIndex tracking. _persist() now short-circuits if state == _lastPersistedIndex (no unsaved changes). save() updates _lastPersistedIndex on success. This narrows the data-loss window to only the gap between the last auto-save and dispose.

bugs.title: seekTo on empty document edge case
bugs.description: If totalWords == 0, seekTo computed clamp(0, -1) which returns 0 in Dart. While not a crash, this set state on an empty document and the timer considered it 'finished'. Added an explicit early-return guard.

tasks.description: Added 'if (state.totalWords == 0) return;' guard at the top of seekTo().

bugs.title: Drift correction causes first word to display at 2x interval
bugs.description: In _scheduleNext(), _lastTick was just set in _startTimer() so elapsed ≈ 0ms, drift = -interval, delay = 2×interval. Every play/resume caused the first word transition to feel sluggish.

tasks.description: Added _isFirstTick flag set true in _startTimer(), cleared in _scheduleNext(). When true, uses raw interval (no drift correction) for the first tick.

potentialIssues.title: ConfigNotifier concurrent mutation race condition
potentialIssues.description: All setter methods read state.valueOrNull, modify, and write back. If two mutations interleave (e.g., updateBookmark + setDefaultWpm), the second read can see stale state from before the first write, losing data.

tasks.description: Added Completer-based async mutex (_lock). All setter methods now go through _synchronized() which serializes concurrent read-modify-write-persist cycles.

potentialIssues.title: Retry timer fires after PreprocessingQueue disposal
potentialIssues.description: Future.delayed in _handleRetry captures 'this'. If notifier is disposed before the delay completes, the 'if (!mounted) return' guard prevents a crash but the retry is silently lost.

tasks.description: Accepted risk — the mounted guard is sufficient. If user navigates away and back, the queue re-initializes and retries from scratch.

potentialIssues.title: PurchaseService.purchasePremium() always returns true
potentialIssues.description: The stub grants premium unconditionally with no receipt validation. Any user can unlock premium for free by triggering the purchase flow.

tasks.description: Added prominent FIXME warnings on the class and method documenting that this must not ship to production without StoreKit/Play Billing integration and server-side receipt verification.

potentialIssues.title: appLog() called inside Isolate.run() context
potentialIssues.description: appLog writes to a main-isolate global list. Calling it from _scanSync (which runs in Isolate.run on non-iOS) either crashes silently or writes to separate isolate memory. Log entries from isolate scans were lost.

tasks.description: Replaced all appLog() calls inside _scanSync with dart:developer dev.log() which works cross-isolate. Added import for dart:developer. Main-isolate callers (scanAsync, pdfListProvider) still use appLog correctly.

potentialIssues.title: Analytics session wordCount can drift from actual words displayed
potentialIssues.description: If Riverpod listener callbacks coalesce during long frames, some currentIndex changes could be missed, under-counting _sessionWordCount. In practice, Riverpod fires per state emission, so this is extremely unlikely.

tasks.description: Accepted risk — Riverpod listeners fire per state emission, not per frame. The drift is theoretical, not observed.

potentialIssues.title: Fullscreen desync on rapid navigation
potentialIssues.description: dispose() always calls SystemChrome.setEnabledSystemUIMode(edgeToEdge) which restores system UI. On rapid navigation there could be a brief flash of incorrect fullscreen state.

tasks.description: Accepted risk — dispose() always restores the correct state. The flash is imperceptible in practice.

potentialIssues.title: Duplicate word selection in range picker picks first occurrence
potentialIssues.description: _findWordIndexOnPage returns the first occurrence of a word on a page. If a common word like 'the' appears multiple times, the wrong range boundary may be selected. A proper fix requires positional data from pdfrx's text selection callback.

tasks.description: Added TODO(P-8) documentation on _findWordIndexOnPage explaining the limitation and noting that positional data from the text selection callback is needed for disambiguation.

manual: BUG-6: Play at 300 WPM, verify first word fires at normal interval (not 2x delay)
BUG-3: Read a PDF, change WPM via dial — verify no full reading screen rebuild
BUG-2: Open PDF A, navigate back, open PDF B — verify no stale words flash
BUG-4: Read for 30 seconds, force-kill app, relaunch — verify bookmark within 5 seconds of last position
BUG-1: Read at 300 WPM — verify bookmark updates exactly once per word advance (check via log output)
P-1: Rapidly change WPM while auto-save timer fires — verify no data loss
